proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Right-sided primary tumor (right_sided_primary=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher CEA (cea_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with different pfs_months than male sex.
Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Linear regression: each unit higher ECOG associated with -1.190 month change in PFS.
Stage IV vs not: PFS coefficient -1.352 months.
Each g/dL higher albumin: 0.465 month PFS change.
Each 1% more weight loss: -0.0730 month PFS change.
Right-sided vs left-sided: -0.305 month PFS change.
Each U/L higher LDH: -0.00032 month PFS change.
Each ng/mL higher CEA: -0.00535 month PFS change.
Female vs male: -0.006 month PFS change.
Each year of age: 0.1765 month PFS change (counter-intuitive positive sign).
Each mg/L higher CRP: -0.00202 month PFS change.
Each unit NLR: 0.0052 month PFS change.
Each g/dL hemoglobin: -0.0101 month PFS change.

proposed_hypotheses.text: KRAS-mutated patients (kras_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
NRAS-mutated patients (nras_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
BRAF V600E-mutated patients (braf_v600e=1) have shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) have different pfs_months than MSI-low patients.
HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) have different pfs_months than HER2 non-amplified.
NTRK fusion-positive patients (ntrk_fusion=1) have different pfs_months than NTRK fusion-negative.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS in KRAS-mutated 4.121 vs KRAS-WT 4.448; difference -0.327 months.
Mean PFS in NRAS-mutated 4.526 vs NRAS-WT 4.305; difference 0.221 months (sign opposite of hypothesis).
Mean PFS in BRAF-V600E 4.094 vs WT 4.322; difference -0.228 months.
Mean PFS MSI-high 4.293 vs MSI-low 4.313; difference -0.019 months (NS).
Mean PFS HER2 amplified 4.258 vs non 4.313; difference -0.056 months (NS).
Mean PFS NTRK-fusion 4.351 vs WT 4.311; difference 0.040 months (NS, n_pos=251).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have different pfs_months than those not receiving cetuximab.
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving bevacizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving encorafenib.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving regorafenib.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS cetuximab 4.285 vs none 4.323; difference -0.038 months (NS).
Mean PFS bev 4.301 vs none 4.320; difference -0.019 months (NS).
Mean PFS pembro 4.316 vs none 4.311; difference 0.005 months (NS).
Mean PFS enco 4.316 vs none 4.311; difference 0.0054 months (NS).
Mean PFS T+T 4.271 vs none 4.315; difference -0.044 months (NS).
Mean PFS rego 5.089 vs none 4.117; difference 0.972 months (highly significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across ECOG performance status categories (0, 1, 2) with monotonically shorter PFS at higher ECOG.

analyses.result_summary: PFS means: ECOG 0 = 5.271, ECOG 1 = 4.054, ECOG 2 = 2.908; ANOVA F=3529.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In MSI-high (msi_high=1) patients, the effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is more positive than in MSI-low patients (i.e., pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in MSI-high).

analyses.result_summary: Treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction coefficient 0.0020 (effect of pembro in MSI-high vs MSI-low). Mean PFS: MSI-high+pembro=4.299 (n=396), MSI-high no pembro=4.292 (n=2117). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In BRAF V600E-mutated (braf_v600e=1) patients, treatment_encorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in BRAF wild-type patients (encorafenib benefit concentrated in BRAF V600E).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.1413. Mean PFS BRAF-V600E+enco=3.979 (n=242), BRAF-V600E no enco=4.108 (n=2030). Hypothesis NOT supported (interaction NS, point estimate negative).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In HER2-amplified (her2_amplified=1) patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces longer pfs_months than in HER2 non-amplified.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient 0.0625, p=0.778. HER2-amp+T+T=4.273 (n=120), HER2-amp no T+T=4.256 (n=1384). NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In KRAS wild-type (kras_mutation=0) patients, treatment_cetuximab produces longer pfs_months than in KRAS-mutated patients (cetuximab benefit concentrated in KRAS WT).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient 0.0196 p=0.665; cetuximab effect in KRAS-WT subgroup -0.048 mo (NS). NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In all-RAS-and-BRAF wild-type and left-sided primary patients, treatment_cetuximab produces longer pfs_months than in those without cetuximab (the canonical benefit subgroup).

analyses.code: ttest in (kras_wt & nras_wt & braf_wt & left_sided) subgroup
analyses.result_summary: In all-WT + left-sided (n_t=5067, n_u=11376): cetuximab vs no cet effect = -0.067 mo (NS). NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_bevacizumab produces longer pfs_months in right-sided primary tumor patients than in left-sided.
Treatment_bevacizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in stage IV patients than non-stage-IV.

analyses.code: ttest_ind bev in right-sided vs left-sided
analyses.result_summary: Bev effect right-sided = -0.0343 mo (NS), left-sided = -0.0123 mo (NS). NOT supported.

analyses.code: ttest_ind bev in stage_iv vs non
analyses.result_summary: Bev effect stage IV = -0.0185 mo (NS), not stage IV = -0.0150 mo (NS). NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_regorafenib produces a larger pfs_months benefit in patients with low CEA (cea_ng_ml at or below median) than in patients with high CEA.
Treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs across ECOG performance status categories.
Treatment_regorafenib effect differs by albumin (low <3.5 vs ≥3.5 g/dL).

analyses.code: ttest rego in low_cea vs high_cea
analyses.result_summary: Rego effect in LOW CEA = 1.806 mo (p<1e-280); in HIGH CEA = 0.135 mo (p=7.75e-05). Strongly supports low-CEA-modified benefit.
Rego effect ECOG 0=0.925, ECOG 1=0.995, ECOG 2=0.896 (all significant, similar magnitude). Heterogeneity not strong.

analyses.code: ttest rego in alb<3.5 vs alb>=3.5
analyses.result_summary: Rego effect alb low = 0.963 mo, alb high = 0.980 mo (both significant, similar). Modest heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant negative interaction between treatment_regorafenib and continuous cea_ng_ml on pfs_months — the regorafenib benefit attenuates as CEA rises.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.0253 per ng/mL CEA (p=8.66e-58); rego effect across CEA tertiles: low=1.784, mid=1.104, high=0.0068 (p=0.865, NS in highest tertile). Strongly supports CEA-modulated benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In KRAS wild-type (kras_mutation=0) patients, treatment_regorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in KRAS-mutated patients (interaction).
In BRAF wild-type (braf_v600e=0) patients, treatment_regorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in BRAF-V600E patients (interaction).
In left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0) patients, treatment_regorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in right-sided patients (interaction).
Treatment_regorafenib × msi_high interaction on pfs_months is significant.
Treatment_regorafenib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months is significant.
Treatment_regorafenib × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months is significant.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -1.629, p=7.69e-226; rego in KRAS-WT effect = 1.656, in KRAS-mut = 0.0269 (NS). STRONGLY supports.
Interaction coef -1.183, p=6.13e-23; rego in BRAF-WT = 1.027, in BRAF-V600E = -0.1556 (NS). STRONGLY supports.
Interaction coef -1.454, p=4.08e-168; rego in left-sided = 1.480, in right-sided = 0.0255 (NS). STRONGLY supports.
MSI interaction coef -0.0219, p=0.850; rego works similarly in MSI-high and MSI-low. NOT supported.
HER2 interaction coef -0.1070, p=0.464; rego works similarly regardless of HER2. NOT supported.
NRAS interaction coef 0.988, p=1.2e-11; rego in NRAS-WT = 0.941, in NRAS-mut = 1.929 (paradoxically larger in NRAS-mut, n=1506). Borderline support, but small subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: None of age_years, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl modify the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months once cea_ng_ml is removed (i.e., these continuous features are not strong heterogeneity drivers for regorafenib).

analyses.code: loop: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * <feature>').fit() for many features
analyses.result_summary: None of age, ECOG, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium produced significant rego × feature interactions (all p>0.10). Only CEA modifies rego effect strongly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable regression of pfs_months on all features and treatments, treatment_regorafenib retains a positive significant coefficient and remains the only treatment with a clinically meaningful main effect.

In a multivariable regression, ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml retain significant adjusted associations with pfs_months.

analyses.code: sm.OLS pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_regorafenib coef = 0.943 (p=0). Other Tx coefs: cetuximab = -0.0110 (p=0.190), bev = 0.0026 (p=0.734), pembro = 0.0003 (p=0.978), enco = 0.0061 (p=0.633), T+T = 0.0258 (p=0.069). R²=0.859. Confirms all features mentioned remain significant; supports h15.1/h15.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In low-CEA + KRAS wild-type patients, treatment_regorafenib produces a much larger pfs_months benefit than in the broader low-CEA group, while in low-CEA + KRAS-mutated patients regorafenib has no detectable benefit.
In low-CEA + left-sided primary patients, treatment_regorafenib produces a much larger pfs_months benefit than in low-CEA + right-sided patients (where the effect is null).
Albumin, ECOG, and stage_iv do not further refine the low-CEA subgroup's regorafenib benefit appreciably.

analyses.code: ttest rego in cea_low & kras_wt vs cea_low & kras_mut
analyses.result_summary: Rego effect in cea_low + kras_wt = 3.065 (n_t=2949, p<<1e-300); in cea_low + kras_mut = 0.0166 (n_t=2073, p=0.748, NS). STRONGLY supports.

analyses.code: ttest rego in cea_low & left_sided vs cea_low & right_sided
analyses.result_summary: Rego effect cea_low+left = 2.741 (n_t=3291, p<<1e-300); cea_low+right = 0.0286 (n_t=1731, p=0.613, NS). STRONGLY supports.

analyses.code: ttest rego in cea_low + ecog<2, alb high, stage_iv variants
analyses.result_summary: Rego in cea_low alone = 1.806; cea_low+ecog<2 = 1.830; cea_low+alb_high = 1.790; cea_low+stage_iv = 1.840; cea_low+not_stage_iv = 1.768. All ~1.8 mo, no major refinement. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even in the canonical cetuximab benefit subgroup (kras_wt & nras_wt & braf_wt & left-sided & msi_low & her2_neg), treatment_cetuximab does not produce a meaningfully positive pfs_months difference in this dataset.

analyses.code: loop ttest cetuximab in many subgroup definitions
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab effect: triple_wt+left = -0.067 (NS, p=0.125); triple_wt+left+msi_low = -0.083 (borderline); triple_wt+left+msi_low+her2_neg (n_t=15078). Effects are slightly NEGATIVE in every refined subgroup; cetuximab shows no benefit. NOT a positive cetuximab subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical features, the treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months remains non-significant.
Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by tumor side (right_sided_primary), with right-sided patients potentially deriving relatively more benefit (or less harm) than left-sided.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembro × msi_high interaction = 0.0522 (p=0.325). Pembro main effect in MSI-low ≈ 0 (p=0.865). NOT supported — no MSI-driven pembro benefit.
Pembro × right_sided_primary interaction = -0.1163 (p=0.050). Borderline; small absolute effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In BRAF V600E + treatment_cetuximab patients, treatment_encorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in BRAF V600E patients without cetuximab (clinical encorafenib + cetuximab combination interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coef = 0.4375 (p=0.237). In BRAF-V600E + cet: enco effect = 0.109 (n_t=58, NS). NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In HER2-amplified + RAS wild-type patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces longer pfs_months than in HER2-amplified + RAS-mutated patients (canonical clinical eligibility).

analyses.code: ttest T+T in (her2_amp & ras_wt) vs (her2_amp & ras_mut)
analyses.result_summary: T+T in HER2+ & RAS-WT effect = 0.052 (n_t=74, p=0.855, NS); HER2+ & RAS-mut = -0.089 (NS). No HER2-driven benefit visible. NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_bevacizumab does not produce a clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit in any clinical subgroup (ECOG, side, CRP, LDH).

analyses.result_summary: Bev effect: ECOG0=0.0255 (NS); ECOG1=-0.0556 (p=0.042 marginally negative); ECOG2=0.0120; right-sided=-0.0343; high CRP=-0.0064. All NS or trivially negative. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the low-CEA subgroup, the regorafenib pfs_months benefit is essentially the same regardless of additional clinical modifiers (ECOG low, albumin high, low weight loss, high hemoglobin, low NLR).

analyses.code: ttest rego across cea_low × ecog_low × alb_high × ... combinations
analyses.result_summary: Rego effect within cea_low & ecog_low & alb_high = 1.795; cea_low & alb_high & wl_low = 1.787; cea_low & alb_high & hb_high = 1.806; cea_low & alb_high & nlr_low = 1.687. All ~1.7-1.8 mo. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: As the count of 'unfavorable' clinical features (high CEA, low albumin, ECOG=2, high weight loss, high LDH) increases from 0 to 5, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months declines monotonically (linear interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Rego × unfavorable_score interaction = -0.376 per added unfavorable feature (p=2.27e-55). Stratified rego effect: score 0 = 1.745, 1 = 1.291, 2 = 0.963, 3 = 0.509, 4 = 0.278, 5 = 0.234. Strongly supported but most of decline is driven by CEA.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the most narrowly defined cetuximab-favorable subgroup (triple WT + left-sided + msi_low + her2_neg + ecog<2), treatment_cetuximab still does NOT produce a positive pfs_months difference; if anything, the point estimate is slightly negative.

analyses.code: ttest cet in narrowly defined favorable subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Cet effect in triple_wt + left + msi_low + her2_neg + ecog<2 = -0.113 (n_t=3991, p=0.020). Slightly negative. SUPPORTED — no cetuximab benefit in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients who are simultaneously LEFT-SIDED (right_sided_primary=0), KRAS WILD-TYPE (kras_mutation=0), BRAF WILD-TYPE (braf_v600e=0), and have LOW CEA (cea_ng_ml at or below the cohort median), treatment_regorafenib produces a substantially LONGER pfs_months than in those without regorafenib; outside this subgroup the regorafenib effect is essentially null. The full subgroup definition is: (right_sided_primary=0) AND (kras_mutation=0) AND (braf_v600e=0) AND (cea_ng_ml ≤ median). The unfavorable values that suppress the regorafenib effect are: right-sided primary, KRAS mutation, BRAF V600E mutation, and high CEA.

None of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces a clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit in any biomarker-defined subgroup in this cohort.
Treatment_regorafenib's pfs_months benefit shrinks linearly as cea_ng_ml increases (continuous interaction), even after adjusting for clinical and molecular covariates.

analyses.code: ttest rego in (left & kras_wt & braf_wt & cea_low) vs ttest rego elsewhere; smf.ols interaction
analyses.result_summary: Rego in [left_sided & kras_wt & braf_wt & cea_low] (n_t=1784, n_u=6872): mean PFS treated = 9.116 vs untreated = 4.083; effect = 5.033 mo (p<<1e-300). Outside subgroup: rego main = 0.093 (p=0.000318). Interaction subgroup × tx coef = 4.940 (p=0). Subgroup-removal checks: same subgroup but cea_high → effect=0.340 (p=5.89e-08); right-sided same 0.110; KRAS-mut same 0.056; BRAF-V600E same -0.241. STRONGLY SUPPORTS.

analyses.code: summary of subgroup screens for non-rego treatments
analyses.result_summary: Across exhaustive screens (treatment × biomarker interactions, treatment × continuous feature interactions, narrow eligibility subgroups), no biomarker subgroup yielded a meaningful positive effect for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or T+T. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ rego * cea_ng_ml + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted rego × cea_ng_ml interaction coef = -0.0258 per ng/mL (p=0). Strongly significant after adjustment. STRONGLY SUPPORTED.
